proposed_hypotheses.text: In this AML cohort the marginal probability of objective_response=1 is below 50%, so it is appropriate to treat objective_response as a rare-ish binary outcome and use logistic regression / proportion-difference tests.
Mean feature_078 (age, range 18-92) does not differ between patients with objective_response=1 versus =0 (i.e., feature_078 has no detectable main effect on the probability of objective_response).

analyses.result_summary: Among N=50000 patients, n=8452 (16.9%) had objective_response=1; outcome is binary and substantially less than 50% prevalent.
Mean feature_078 was 68.03 in responders vs 67.98 in non-responders (t-test p=0.7183); univariate logistic log-OR per SD = 0.0043 (p=0.7183). No detectable age effect on objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response rate is higher in patients with feature_035=1 than in patients with feature_035=0 (positive association between feature_035 and objective_response).
The objective_response rate is higher in patients with feature_093=1 than in patients with feature_093=0 (positive association between feature_093 and objective_response).
The objective_response rate is lower in patients with feature_121=1 than in patients with feature_121=0 (negative association between feature_121 and objective_response).

analyses.result_summary: objective_response rate is 22.1% in feature_035=1 vs 16.5% in feature_035=0 (absolute difference +5.6 pts; chi-square p=3.52e-18). Strong positive association supported.
objective_response rate is 18.5% in feature_093=1 vs 16.8% in feature_093=0 (diff +1.7 pts; p=0.0056).
objective_response rate is 15.6% in feature_121=1 vs 17.0% in feature_121=0 (diff -1.4 pts; p=0.0249).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_011 (continuous, range 0-23.6) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response=1 (negative log-odds slope per SD increase in feature_011).
Higher feature_006 (continuous, range 20-100) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response=1 (negative log-odds slope per SD increase in feature_006).
Higher feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) is associated with a higher probability of objective_response=1 (positive log-odds slope per SD increase in feature_099).
Higher feature_063 (continuous, range 0.03-300) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response=1.
Higher feature_092 (continuous, range 0.5-500) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response=1.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic: log-OR per SD of feature_011 = -0.1550 (p=7.43e-35); strongly negative.
Univariate logistic: log-OR per SD of feature_006 = -0.1086 (p=1.16e-19); strongly negative.
Univariate logistic: log-OR per SD of feature_099 = 0.0577 (p=1.36e-06); positive.
Univariate logistic: log-OR per SD of feature_063 = -0.0642 (p=2.82e-06); negative.
Univariate logistic: log-OR per SD of feature_092 = -0.0560 (p=1.88e-05); negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (3-level ordinal: 0/1/2) shows a monotonic decrease in the probability of objective_response=1 as the feature value increases from 0 to 2 (negative ordinal log-odds slope).
feature_018 (ordinal, 0-10) is not associated with objective_response (no monotonic trend).
feature_045, feature_096, and feature_125 (each 0-4 ordinal) are not strongly associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_057: 0=21.3% (n=17592), 1=15.5% (n=24971), 2=11.2% (n=7437). Ordinal logistic log-OR per unit = -0.3837 (p=1.84e-97). Strong, monotonic decrease.
Ordinal logistic log-OR per unit feature_018 = 0.0042 (p=0.3399). No detectable trend.
feature_045 log-OR per unit = -0.0195 (p=0.0695); feature_096 log-OR per unit = 0.0068 (p=0.5145); feature_125 log-OR per unit = -0.0233 (p=0.0383). All effects are small; none significant at p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_005='black' have a lower probability of objective_response=1 than patients with feature_005='white' (reference).
The omnibus 5-level test of feature_005 (white/asian/black/hispanic/other) versus objective_response is statistically significant (the response rate is not constant across feature_005 categories).
feature_087 (insurance: medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured) is not associated with objective_response (no detectable category effect).

analyses.result_summary: 5x2 chi-square across feature_005 levels: chi2=13.37, p=0.0096. Response rates: white=17.3%, hispanic=16.7%, asian=16.3%, black=15.7%, other=14.7%.

Black vs white log-OR = -0.1120 (p=0.0038); asian vs white = -0.0683 (p=0.1873); hispanic vs white = -0.0418 (p=0.2195); other vs white = -0.1908 (p=0.0338). Black and 'other' are nominally lower than white.
4x2 chi-square across feature_087 levels: chi2=2.16, p=0.5403. No category significantly differs from private; insurance does not predict objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a single logistic regression for all 127 features (including race and insurance dummies), feature_057 remains a significant negative predictor of objective_response.
After full multivariable adjustment, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, and feature_092 each remain independent negative predictors of objective_response.
After full multivariable adjustment, feature_035 and feature_099 remain independent positive predictors of objective_response.
After full multivariable adjustment, feature_078 (age) remains non-significant for objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-OR per unit of feature_057 = -0.3845 (p=8.50e-97); the dominant predictor in the full model.

Adjusted log-OR per SD: feature_011 = -0.1560 (p=1.22e-34); feature_006 = -0.1114 (p=3.02e-20); feature_063 = -0.0661 (p=1.70e-06); feature_092 = -0.0587 (p=8.83e-06). All four remain negative and significant.
Adjusted log-OR: feature_035 (binary) = 0.3601 (p=3.17e-17); feature_099 per SD = 0.0589 (p=1.11e-06); feature_093 (binary) = 0.1314 (p=0.0022). Each remains positive and significant.
Adjusted log-OR per SD feature_078 = 0.0031 (p=0.7971); age remains non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the top clinical predictors of objective_response (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_093), the lower objective_response rate in feature_005='black' versus 'white' is no longer statistically significant (i.e., the race difference is largely explained by the clinical features).

analyses.code: Logit(y, top-8 standardized predictors + race dummies (white reference))
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted black vs white log-OR = -0.0300 (p=0.6273); adjusted other vs white log-OR = -0.1156 (p=0.2594). Joint 4-d.f. test of all race dummies after adjustment p=0.0098. The univariate race difference attenuates after clinical adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_057, such that the log-OR of feature_035 differs significantly across feature_057 strata (a non-zero feature_035 × feature_057 interaction term).

analyses.result_summary: Stratum-specific feature_035 log-OR: feature_057=0 0.340 (p=1.54e-07), feature_057=1 0.368 (p=2.38e-09), feature_057=2 0.393 (p=0.0022). Interaction term log-OR = 0.0270 (p=0.6736). feature_035 has a similar positive effect across all feature_057 strata; no significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_005 (race), so that the log-OR of feature_035 differs significantly across the five race categories (joint feature_035 × feature_005 interaction non-zero).

analyses.code: Logit(y, [feature_035, race dummies (white ref), feature_035*race dummies]); joint F-test on all interaction terms
analyses.result_summary: Per-race feature_035 log-OR: white=0.406 (p=2.62e-15), black=0.285 (p=0.0253), hispanic=0.274 (p=0.0120), asian=0.269 (p=0.1269), other=0.386 (p=0.2021). Joint interaction F-test p=0.7285. No evidence the feature_035 effect varies by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_087 (insurance), so the feature_035 log-OR differs significantly across medicaid/medicare/private/uninsured (joint feature_035 × feature_087 interaction non-zero).

analyses.result_summary: Per-insurance feature_035 log-OR: medicare=0.459 (p=9.55e-13), medicaid=0.454 (p=5.33e-05), private=0.237 (p=0.0004), uninsured=0.375 (p=0.0775). Joint interaction F-test p=0.0929.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_078 (age), with the feature_035 log-OR differing across age tertiles (a non-zero feature_035 × feature_078 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Age-tertile feature_035 log-OR: low (18-63y) 0.326 (p=8.64e-06); mid (63-73y) 0.402 (p=2.03e-08); high (73-92y) 0.363 (p=6.56e-07). Continuous age × feature_035 interaction log-OR = -0.0042 (p=0.9195); no detectable age modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effects of feature_006 and feature_011 on the log-odds of objective_response are super-additive: the feature_006 × feature_011 product term has a non-zero coefficient (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR (per SD×SD) = 0.0211 (p=0.0934). Marginal positive interaction; not strictly significant at p<0.05. The two main effects remain strongly negative independently.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 on objective_response depends on the level of feature_057 (a non-zero feature_057 × feature_011 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR per SD×unit = -0.0072 (p=0.7099). No detectable interaction; the feature_011 slope is essentially the same across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_006 on objective_response depends on the level of feature_057 (a non-zero feature_057 × feature_006 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR per SD×unit = -0.0179 (p=0.3300). No detectable interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effects of feature_035 and feature_093 on objective_response combine super-multiplicatively: the feature_035 × feature_093 interaction term in a logistic model has a positive non-zero coefficient (their joint effect is larger than the product of individual ORs).

analyses.result_summary: Joint response rates: f035=0/f093=0 16.4% (n=42646); f035=1/f093=0 21.8% (n=3329); f035=0/f093=1 17.9% (n=3730); f035=1/f093=1 25.8% (n=295). Interaction log-OR = 0.1103 (p=0.4519). No significant interaction; effects are approximately multiplicative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative slope of feature_011 on the log-odds of objective_response differs significantly across feature_005 (race) categories (a joint feature_005 × feature_011 interaction).

analyses.code: Logit(y, [z(feature_011) + race dummies + z*race products]); F-test on interactions
analyses.result_summary: Per-race log-OR per SD feature_011: white=-0.149 (p=5.23e-22, n=32498); black=-0.171 (p=6.36e-06, n=5889); hispanic=-0.153 (p=2.33e-06, n=7631); asian=-0.203 (p=0.0001, n=2970); other=-0.124 (p=0.1803, n=1012). Joint interaction F-test p=0.9006; the feature_011 slope is similar in all racial groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative slope of feature_011 on the log-odds of objective_response is attenuated in feature_087='uninsured' compared with the other insurance categories (joint feature_087 × feature_011 interaction non-zero, with uninsured stratum showing a substantially smaller negative slope).

analyses.code: Logit(y, [z(feature_011) + ins dummies + z*ins products]); F-test
analyses.result_summary: Per-insurance log-OR per SD feature_011: medicare=-0.184 (p=5.05e-21, n=20953); private=-0.141 (p=1.36e-12, n=20090); medicaid=-0.145 (p=1.65e-05, n=6911); uninsured=-0.041 (p=0.5005, n=2046). Joint interaction F-test p=0.0822; the uninsured slope is closer to zero but the joint test does not reach p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_011 on objective_response is stronger in older patients (i.e., a negative feature_078 × feature_011 interaction in a logistic model).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR (per SD×SD) = -0.0231 (p=0.0657); the negative slope of feature_011 is slightly more negative in older patients but the term is not significant at p<0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_006 on objective_response is stronger in older patients (negative feature_078 × feature_006 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR = 0.0005 (p=0.9667). No detectable age modification of the feature_006 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_057 on objective_response is modified by feature_078 (age), with feature_057 having a different log-OR per unit in older versus younger patients (non-zero feature_078 × feature_057 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR per SD×unit = 0.0240 (p=0.1890). No detectable age modification of the feature_057 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effects of feature_011 and feature_063 on objective_response are super-additive (negative feature_011 × feature_063 interaction term in a logistic model).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR per SD×SD = -0.0260 (p=0.0919). Marginal negative interaction; not significant at p<0.05. Both main effects remain robustly negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_035 on objective_response is modified by feature_006, such that feature_035 has a different log-OR at higher versus lower feature_006 (non-zero feature_006 × feature_035 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction log-OR per SD×unit = 0.0357 (p=0.3875). No detectable interaction; feature_035 helps similarly across the feature_006 range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a parsimonious 8-variable logistic model containing only feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, and feature_093, all eight predictors retain the same direction of effect as in the univariate analyses and remain statistically significant (p<0.05).

analyses.code: Logit(y, the eight standardized top predictors)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across cells defined by feature_057 × feature_005 (race), the objective_response rate is consistently higher in patients with feature_035=1 than in those with feature_035=0 (the feature_035 effect generalizes across the joint feature_057 × race subgroup structure with no significant 3-way interaction).

analyses.code: Logit(y, feature_035 + feature_057 + race + 2-way and 3-way products); joint F-test on race × feature_035 × feature_057 terms
analyses.result_summary: Cells (f057 × race) all showed higher f035=1 response rates than f035=0 (15 evaluated cells). Examples: f057=0,race=white: f035=1 RR=28.5% (n=835), f035=0 RR=21.2% (n=10566); f057=0,race=asian: f035=1 RR=22.5% (n=89), f035=0 RR=20.2% (n=996); f057=0,race=black: f035=1 RR=25.5% (n=161), f035=0 RR=20.0% (n=1902); f057=0,race=hispanic: f035=1 RR=24.4% (n=213), f035=0 RR=20.6% (n=2471); f057=0,race=other: f035=1 RR=24.2% (n=33), f035=0 RR=16.3% (n=326); f057=1,race=white: f035=1 RR=21.2% (n=1172), f035=0 RR=15.4% (n=15098). Joint 3-way interaction F-test p=0.9312; feature_035 acts as a robust positive marker across the feature_057 × race grid.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious logistic model using only the eight strongest predictors (feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_035, feature_099, feature_063, feature_092, feature_093) has an in-sample AUC for objective_response that is substantially higher than 0.5 and substantially higher than the AUC achievable from feature_078 (age) alone.

The full 127-predictor logistic model offers only a marginal AUC improvement over the parsimonious 8-predictor model, indicating that most of the signal in the dataset is captured by these eight features.

analyses.result_summary: AUC age-only = 0.5007; AUC parsimonious 8-var model = 0.5972; difference 0.0965. The parsimonious model substantially outperforms an age-only model.
AUC parsimonious (8 vars) = 0.5972; AUC full 127-var model = 0.6040; full-vs-parsimonious AUC delta = 0.0068. Most of the predictive signal is concentrated in the eight identified features.
